scenes.description: Jitterbug dance contest with Diane and grandparents; transitions to first-person POV falling onto pillow
scenes.notes: Memory/reality fragment that bookends the dream. Per analysis: 'innocent persona of past' that gets inserted into dream

scenes.description: Camilla in limo says 'We don't stop here'; assassination attempt foiled by car accident; she escapes injured and amnesiac
scenes.notes: In real timeline this is the limo ride to Adam's party; transformed in dream into assassination

scenes.description: Dan tells Herb about recurring dream of a man behind Winkie's; they investigate; Dan collapses in fear seeing the bum
scenes.notes: Pure dream-logic scene. Dan is a character Diane saw at Winkie's when meeting hitman

scenes.description: Wheelchair-bound Mr. Roque issues unspoken commands via phone chain about 'the girl'
scenes.notes: Hollywood power broker persona; symbolic of unyielding control

scenes.description: Betty arrives at LAX from Deep River Ontario, cheerfully says goodbye to elderly couple Irene and her husband who later laugh maniacally
scenes.notes: Innocent persona enters the dream world. Note the 'sinister laugh' foreshadowing

scenes.description: Injured amnesiac Rita walks down Sunset Boulevard at dawn, hides in bushes, sneaks into apartment
scenes.notes: Mirrors William Mulholland water flowing 'down hill into LA'

scenes.description: Detectives Harry and Neal investigate Mulholland Drive crash; find earring suggesting missing person
scenes.notes: Brief scene; detective figures recurring throughout

scenes.scene_title: Rita Hides in Aunt Ruths Apartment
scenes.description: Rita sneaks past aunt Ruth who is leaving for Canada, hides under coffee table then in bedroom
scenes.notes: Aunt Ruth's apartment becomes safe haven in dream

scenes.description: Coco welcomes Betty to courtyard apartment; Betty discovers Rita in shower; Rita claims her name is Rita seeing 'Gilda' poster
scenes.notes: Two key personas meet. Rita takes name from Rita Hayworth poster

scenes.description: Mob brothers force director Adam to cast 'this is the girl' Camilla Rhodes (blonde); Luigi spits espresso into napkin
scenes.notes: Critical 'this is the girl' line - parallels Mulholland's 'There it is, take it'

scenes.description: After Adam refuses, Mr. Roque orders 'Shut everything down'
scenes.notes: Suicide metaphor - 'shut everything down' is Diane contemplating suicide

scenes.description: Hitman Joe accidentally kills Ed and two bystanders trying to steal the black book containing prostitution call list
scenes.notes: Joe is the hitman Diane hires - revealed as comically incompetent in dream

scenes.scene_title: Betty Calls Aunt Ruth / Rita Wakes
scenes.description: Betty pretends to Aunt Ruth that all is well, decides to help Rita find her identity
scenes.notes: Betty embraces investigation 'like in the movies'

scenes.description: Adam comes home, finds wife Lorraine in bed with pool man Gene; pours pink paint in her jewelry box
scenes.notes: Adam getting humiliated mirrors Diane's humiliation

scenes.description: Adam learns from secretary Cynthia he's been fired and is broke
scenes.notes: Adam loses everything - dream punishment

scenes.description: Betty and Rita see news about accident; phone tracing scene; phone call to D. Hingle (Diane Selwyn)
scenes.notes: Phone call to Diane's actual apartment from dream

scenes.description: The Cowboy delivers cryptic ultimatum: 'You will see me one more time if you do good. You will see me two more times if you do bad'
scenes.notes: Mythic figure - represents Diane's attempt at smarter persona

scenes.description: Rita reveals to Betty huge amount of cash and a strange blue key in her purse
scenes.notes: Blue key central symbol introduced - signifies Camilla's death in real world

scenes.description: Betty does shockingly powerful audition with sleazy actor Woody for Wally's project; impresses casting director Linney
scenes.notes: Reveals childhood sexual abuse subtext: 'I hate you. I hate us both!' - script implies abuse by father's friend

scenes.scene_title: Set of The Sylvia North Story
scenes.description: Linney brings Betty to Adam's set; Adam locks eyes with Betty; Camilla Rhodes (blonde) lip-syncs '16 Reasons'
scenes.notes: Adam choosing the wrong Camilla - dream revenge for real-world rejection

scenes.description: Betty and Rita investigate Diane Selwyn's apartment, knock on door of #17 then #12
scenes.notes: Apartments switched - this is the swap with neighbor

scenes.description: Betty and Rita find rotting corpse on bed - Diane's dead body, foreshadowing the suicide ending
scenes.notes: Same bed as opening pillow shot. Diane sees herself dead

scenes.description: Rita transforms into Betty's double with blonde wig; they make love
scenes.notes: Rita merges with Betty - identity dissolving

scenes.description: Magician declares 'No hay banda - it is all an illusion'; Rebekah del Rio sings 'Llorando' (Crying); Betty finds blue box in purse
scenes.notes: Climactic revelation scene - 'It's all illusion'. del Rio collapses while singing continues

scenes.description: Betty disappears as Rita opens blue box; box falls empty; Aunt Ruth checks empty room
scenes.notes: Dream ends. Betty/Diane personality dissolves. Blue box = locked truth

scenes.description: The Cowboy says 'Hey pretty girl, time to wake up'; Diane wakes wearing same robe
scenes.notes: Reality begins. Same pillow as opening fall. Cowboy's 'one more time if good'

scenes.description: Diane sees Camilla on couch then realizes hallucination; grumpy neighbor returns to retrieve dishes
scenes.notes: Lynch's clue #6: notice robe, ashtray, coffee cup

scenes.description: Flashback: Camilla kisses Adam on set in front of Diane; humiliation
scenes.notes: The real moment of betrayal

scenes.description: Camilla breaks up with Diane on couch / Diane forces herself on Camilla
scenes.notes: Forced sexual encounter / breakup

scenes.description: Diane masturbates while crying; bitter emotion
scenes.notes: Self-destructive scene. Lynch clue: 'watch what Diane is wearing'

scenes.description: Phone rings - Camilla invites Diane to Adam's house party. Limo arrives
scenes.notes: Lynch clue #2: Notice red lampshade. Limo ride that becomes the dream's opening

scenes.description: Diane in limo, says 'We don't stop here'; Camilla appears, leads her up secret path
scenes.notes: Same words as dream limo scene; this is the actual event

scenes.description: Diane meets Adam's mom Coco; Camilla and the blonde Camilla kiss; engagement announcement; the Cowboy walks past; Diane breaks down crying
scenes.notes: All the dream personas seen as real people; engagement is the trigger

scenes.description: Diane hires hitman Joe; he says 'when it's done you'll find this' showing blue key; Dan looks over
scenes.notes: This is what triggers Dan's dream sequence at start of film

scenes.scene_title: Diane Returns to Apartment / Sees Key
scenes.description: Diane sits on couch, sees blue key on coffee table - the murder is done
scenes.notes: Realization moment - Camilla is actually dead

scenes.description: Old couple emerge as demons from blue box; chase Diane to bedroom; she shoots herself in head; smoke appears
scenes.notes: Suicide with gun. Demons from blue box represent abusive past

scenes.description: Final image: Betty and Rita appear over LA skyline; blue-haired lady whispers 'Silencio'
scenes.notes: Closing word. Hamlet's 'rest is silence'. End

characters.notes: Central protagonist; Betty = idealized past self; Diane = real broken self
Brunette in dream; same actress plays both
Treated harshly in dream; engaged to Camilla in reality
Maternal figure in both worlds
Brief dream appearance; in reality police are searching
Sees Diane meeting hitman; saw the blue key
Possibly Diane's psychiatrist
Already dead in reality; her money funded Diane's LA dream
Diane sees her own corpse in the dream
Different actress same character name; ironic doubling
Killed in dream; succeeds in reality
Three appearances: pretty good if good once etc.
Wheelchair-bound silent ruler
Brothers force the casting
Famously spits espresso into napkin
Tells Coco 'someone is in trouble'
Says final word 'Silencio'
Same actor plays both - dream doubling
Sings 'Llorando' - song continues after she falls
Holds blue box; emerges as Diane's repressed self
Same couple at airport laugh maniacally then return as demons
'It'll do'
Connection to Aunt Ruth
Audition scene reveals abuse subtext

symbols.notes: Found in Betty's purse at Silencio; opens to nothingness; demons emerge in real world
Hitman tells Diane she'll find this when 'it's done'; same key in dream signifies repressed truth
Lynch clue #2: Notice the red lampshade. Recurring lit moments
Lynch clue #6: Notice the robe, ashtray, coffee cup. Always present at Diane's couch
Lynch clue #6 - present in Diane's reality scenes
Lynch clue #6 - what Diane is wearing
Rita Hayworth poster in bathroom - origin of Rita name
Won contest in Deep River Ontario - mentioned at dinner party
Mr. Roque's phone chain; Camilla calling Diane to party
Same words 'we don't stop here' in both
Rita transforms into Betty's double
Same money bag in dream and reality - what hitman is paid
Red curtains at Club Silencio
Site of murder pact and dream haunting
Behind Winkies; emerges from blue box; appears in Diane's dying face
In bedside drawer
Camera falls onto pillow at start; Diane wakes on it; dies on it
Audition script with abuse undertones
Photo of blonde Camilla shown to Adam
Luigi spits espresso into napkin
Adam pours pink paint in wife's jewelry box
Apartment directory shows D Hingle / D Selwyn
Falling dishes near end metaphor
Adam's wife's jewelry destroyed
Cowboy persona's signature
'Crying' in Spanish - continues after singer falls
'There is no band - it is all an illusion'
Said by Castigliane to force casting; said by Diane to hitman
Said by magician and blue-haired lady
Theater of illusion

dialogue.dialogue: What are you doing? We don't stop here!
on the floor in back of the Caddy.
Any of those dead kids wearin' pearl earrings?
No. Could be someone's missin' maybe.
Just one more bag after that.
I just wanted to come here.
I had a dream about this place.
Okay, so you had a dream about this place.
it's the second one I've had..
but they are both the same..
they start out that I'm in here but..
It's kinda half night, you know?
But it looks just like this..
And I'm scared like I can't tell ya.
You're in both dreams and you're scared.
I get even more frightened when I see how afraid you are and..
then I realize what it is.
He's the one who's doing it.
I can see him through the wall.
I hope that I never see that face ever..
you came to see if he's out there.
To get rid of this god-awful feeling.
Dan? You all right? Dan? Dan?
It's time to say goodbye, Betty.
It's been so nice travelling with you.
Thank you, Irene. I was so excited and nervous.
It was sure great to have you to talk to.
Remember I'll be watching for you on the big screen.
Okay Irene. Won't that be the day.
Take care of yourself and be careful.
Betty, it was so nice meeting you All the luck in the world.
It is Mrs. Lenoix isn't it?
Oh, in all my living glory, baby.
- Pleased to meet you. - Just call me Coco.
Everybody else does. Wait there, and I'll go get the key.
If that damned dog craps in the courtyard one more time..
I'm just gonna bake his little butt for breakfast.
- You don't have pets do you? - No, I don't.
They're allowed, I just don't like 'em here..
You know, there was a man that lived here once..
that had a prize fighting kangaroo.
what that kangaroo did to this courtyard.
Let's see your Aunt's apartment ..it's a good one.
Well, you and your Aunt probably have an understanding, so here's the key.
And if you need anything, just give a holler.
Everybody in this building's pretty much okay with me..
If you like, later on I'll introduce you around.
No hard feelings if you don't.
Oh my! I'm sorry. My Aunt Ruth didn't tell me someone was going to be here.
I'm Ruth's niece. My name's Betty.
I'm sure she told you I was coming.
I understand. I saw the dress.
I'll let you get back to that. I'm going to get unpacked.
I'm sorry. I'll get out of here and we can talk later.
do you work with my Aunt?
She's letting me stay here while she's working on a movie..
but I guess you already know that.
I couldn't afford a place like this in a million years.
Unless of course I'm discovered and become a movie star.
Of course, I'd rather be known as a great actress than a movie star,
but sometimes people end up being both
so that is, I guess you'd say, sort of why I came here.
I'm sorry, I'm just so excited to be here..
I mean I just came here from Deep River, Ontario..
You can imagine how I feel.
- We should call a doctor. - No
- But, this could be serious. - No, I..
If you have a concussion you shouldn't sleep.
It will feel okay if I sleep.
I just need to lie down here and sleep.
You see Adam.. there are some suggestions
that are to be brought forward..
and I know you said you would entertain suggestions
and that's all anybody here is asking you to do.
You're in the process of re-casting your lead actress and
We're asking you to keep an open mind.
OK. Let's try the next one.
This is Mr. Darby, whom you know
and this is the director Adam Kesher..
May I offer you, gentlemen.. anything?
- What's the photo for? - An espresso.
I think you're gonna enjoy your espresso this time.
I've done quite a bit of a research knowing how hard you are to please.
- It's not a recommendation. - This is the girl.
What girl? For what? What is this, Ray?
We'd be happy to put her on the list for consideration.
You'd be pleased to know there's quite a bit of interest in this role.
There's six of the top actresses that want this thing.
- Ray, take care of this. - Hold on, Adam.
There's no way. There's no way!
Sorry! That was a highly recommended..
That's considered one of the finest espressos in the world, Sir.
What's going on here? There's no way that girl is in my movie.
Hey! That girl is not in my film!
Did the Castigliane brothers get out of that limo?
- The two guys in dark suits? - Yeah.
an accident like that.. who could have foreseen that?
So, bro.. God you look good!
Just doin' some stuff for this guy.
Hey, man, it's not that bad.
I hope you're not goin' to get in any trouble.
- It was a funny story. - Fucking car accident.
The history of the world in phone numbers.
She's hurt bad! Could you come here and call on the phone. Please I need you!
She's hurt bad! You gotta call the hospital
Come on man! I'm serious about this!
I can't do everything by myself!
I'm gonna study those lines until I know them inside out.
Yep, either here on this fabulous leather couch..
or I'll take them with a coffee in the courtyard..
Oh! I got a real shock to find your friend Rita here.
She was in the shower and I opened the door..
I'm sure there's some explanation for this Aunt Ruth,
I don't think we have to do that.
Please don't worry.. we don't need the police.
I thought you knew my Aunt and that's why you were here.
I just talked to my Aunt on the phone and she wants me to call the police.
I thought when I woke up..
I thought sleep would do it..
I don't know who I am.
What do you mean? You're Rita.
I'm not. I don't know what my name is.
I don't know who I am!
This is your purse. Your name must be in your purse.
You want to know, don't you?
- I.. yes, but.. I.. - Open it!
- You sure you don't want anything? - Not here.. no thanks.
I'll take a cigarette if you have one.
Sure, reach inside my shirt pocket there.
Any new girls on the street lately?
You'll keep your eyes open for me won't you baby?
You don't know where it came from?
When you think of them.. the money.. the key..
does it make you remember anything?
Ray did! Please I'm talking on the phone,
and then they closed the set.. everybody's gone.
You'd better get down here Adam!
You've got to talk to Ray you've got to fix this.
Meet me at the office. We've got to do something..
you've got to do something Adam!
- There must be something we can do. - I'm going home.
I wonder where you were going.
Maybe that's where the accident was.
There must be a police report. We could call..
We could call anonymously from a pay phone..
just to see if there was an accident.
Come on. It'll be just like in the movies.
We'll pretend to be someone else.
I want to walk around anyway.
I'm in Hollywood and I haven't even seen any of it.
Come on Rita. Do you feel up to it?
Just to see if there was an accident..
Just forget you ever saw. It's better that way.
What the hell are you even doing here?
What're you doin' You're hurting me!
That's not a way to treat your wife, buddy.
I don't care what she's done.
I'm inquiring about an accident that happened last night on Mulholland Drive.
Hold please. I'll connect you to traffic.
well, I heard a sound last night that sounded sort of like a carcrash and
I want to know if there was an accident on Mulholland Drive.
- Can you tell me what happened? - No. I can't.
- Was anyone hurt? - May I have your name please?
He wouldn't tell me anything else, but that was your accident Rita.
Maybe there's something about it in the papers.
Come on we'll buy a cup of coffee and we can see.
Maybe it happened so late it's not going to be in today's paper.
That'd be all? You want your check?
- No. Just the coffee. - I'll take our check.
D. Selwyn.. it's the only one.
It's strange to be calling yourself.
Maybe that isn't Diane Selwyn's voice.
or if it is Diane Selwyn..
she can tell you who you are.
- Yeah, right.. get lost! - Is this Adam Kesher's house?
Like hell it is! Get out.. NOW!
Get out of here! He's never coming back! Ever!
I guess you don't understand English. She said to leave.
- Yeah.. what is it, Cookie? - I'm sorry Mr. Kesher..
but there seems tobe some problem with your credit cards.
What? I pay you cash, Cookie.
Fact is two guys from your bank came by.
- How'd they know I was here? - I don't know.
I'm afraid they said you're maxed out at your bank..
and your line of credit has been cancelled.
I don't know what's going on.
I've got enough cash for you Cookie.
It was my duty to inform you.
People you're hiding from, they know where you are.
Someone's maybe shut off my money!
I know. Where are you Adam?
What do you mean you know?
Somebody called. When they couldn't get you..
they told me you were as good as broke.
I didn't believe them, so I made a few calls.
I know, but you're broke. Where are you?
Do you know somebody called the "The Cowboy"?
This guy, the Cowboy, wants to see you.
Jason said he thought it would be a good idea.
Oh, Jason thought it would be a good idea for me to go to see the Cowboy.
Should I wear my ten gallon hat and my six shooters?
Listen. Something tells me this guy is connected to what's happening,
and I think you should do it and I think you should do it right away.
It's been a very strange day..
Where do I meet this Cowboy? I mean.. do I have to ride out to the range?
If I tell him the meeting's on..
you have to go to the top of Beachwood Canyon.
There's a corral up there where he'll be.
You've got to be kidding me!
It's been that kind of a day. When?
You could stay at my place, if you want it.
Cynthia.. I don't think it'll be a good idea.
I'm just offering a place to stay.
And I appreciate the offer, Cynthia. I'll find a place.
Now go on and give the Cowboy a yodel and get back to me.
Okay, but you don't know what you're missing.
Just get along little dowgie and call me back.
It's right about here on Sierra Bonita.
I know you're afraid of something.
What are you doing in Ruth's apartment?
I'm her niece. My name's Betty.
Someone is in trouble. Something bad is happening!
I'm sorry, but I don't know who you are and..
Louise.. What are you doing Louise?
Oh, Coco! I've been trying to find you all afternoon since 3:00 o'clock.
That one is in my room and she won't leave.
I want you to get her out. I want you to get her out now.
This is Louise Bonner. I'm sure she meant well.
Louise, this is Betty, this is Ruth's niece.
In fact and fortunately I was just on my way here to see Betty.
Betty's a young actress and I was just delivering..
some faxed pages of a scene for a big audition tomorrow.
- Well, here they are honey. - Oh, thank you.
Now come along Louise and I'll take you home.
I'm sorry. It happens sometimes. Come on.
No, she said it was someone else who was in trouble.
Stop it, Louise. I'm taking you home..
Sure want to thank ya for coming all the way up to see me..
No problem. What's on your mind?
Well now, here's a man who wants to get right down to it.
Kinda anxious to get to it are ya?
a man's attitude goes some ways
the way his life will be.
Is that somethin' you might agree with?
Now.. did you answer because you thought that's what I wanted to hear..
or did you think about what I said..
and answer cause you truly believe that to be right?
I agree with what you said.. truly.
That a man's attitude determines to a large extent how his life will be.
you must be a person who does not care about the good life.
Well, stop for a little second and think about it.
Can you do that for me?
You're too busy being a smart aleck to be thinkin'.
Now I want ya to think and stop bein' a smart aleck.
Can you try that for me?
Look.. where's this going? What do you want me to do?
How many drivers does a buggy have?
So let's just say I'm drivin' this buggy..
and you if fix your attitude you can ride along with me.
I want you to go back to work tomorrow.
You were re-casting the lead actress anyway..
audition many girls for the part.
When you see the girl that was shown to you earlier today, you will say:
The rest of the cast can stay.
But that lead girl is not up to you.
Now, you will see me one more time..
You will see me two more times..
I came back. I thought that's what you wanted.
My parents are right upstairs! They think you've left.
- So.. surprise.. - I can call them.. I can call my dad..
If you're trying to blackmail me.. it's not going to work.
You're playing a dangerous game here.
You know what I want.. it's not that difficult.
Get out! Get out before I call my dad..
he trusts you.. you're his best friend.
This will be the end of everything.
What about you? What will your dad think about you?
Stop! Just stop! That's what you said from the beginning.
If I tell what happened.. they'll arrest you and put you in jail.
So get out of here before..
Then they'd put you in jail.
Then I cry, cry, cry, and then I say with big emotion:
"I hate you.. I hate us both!"
Betty, can I see you outside for a minute?
- What is it Coco? - Your aunt called
She wants to know who's staying in her apartment.
It's just for a night or two, until she finds her own place.
I tried to explain that to Aunt Ruth, but the connection was bad..
and her plane was leaving, and she got it all mixed up.
I kept telling her it was my friend Rita..
and she kept saying she didn't know any Ritas..
What you're telling me is a load of horse-pucky
even though it comes from a good place.
Now, I'm gonna trust you to sort this thing out
- Thank you. - Don't make me out to be a sucker.
Louise Bonner says there's trouble in there.
but if there is trouble get rid of it.
Is it bad for you that I'm here?
Now, I've gotta fix that sandwich!
And my audition is in one hour.
I hope I'm back in a couple of hours.
- Don't drink all the coke. - Good luck!
When I get back I'll have the cab waiting, so be ready to go.
Betty Elms, I'd like you to meet..
Woody Katz who we've cast in the part..
of "Chuck". He'll do the scene with you.
Alas we can't afford her to cast our show,
Why thank you, Wally. Hello Betty.
Oh, and the ever lovely Martha,
Martha Johnson, who you met out front.
So shall we get it to go?
Would you like a water or a coffee before we begin?
Well then, let's everyone take a seat.
Betty, why don't you join Woody over there and we'll play the scene.
Bob, do you have anything you wish to say..
something to Betty before we begin?
the two of them with themselves,
so don't play it for real..
Just tell me where it hurts, baby.
Hey Bobby, I want to play this one nice and close,
like we did with that other girl,
what's her name? The one with the black hair.
Just don't rush that line again.
I told you.. the line where you say, "Before what?"
I just play off 'em. They all say:
"put you in jail".. They all say it the same way,
Yeah.. Betty, look.. You don't rush it. I don't rush it.
We're gonna play it nice and close..
just like in the movies. OK?
Dad's best friend goes to work.
I came back. I thought that's what you wanted.
My parents are right upstairs! They think you've left.
I can call them.. I can call my dad..
You're playing a dangerous game here..
if you're trying to blackmail me..
Get out before I call my dad.
This will be the end of everything.
What about you? What will your dad think about you?
That's what you said from the beginning.
If I tell them what happened.. they'll arrest you..
Then they'd put you in jail.
- I'm going to take her over there. - Yeah big time!
Baby, you got away with it!
I mean it was forced maybe..
and I really mean that sincerely.
and I'm going to tell her the first chance I get.
I'm sure you all have a lot to talk about. We'll walk Betty out.
Well, yes indeed. And it was our pleasure, Linney.
Don't you be a stranger around here.
- We'd love to have you here. - Come on, Betty.
It was nice meeting all of you.
Not you Betty. You were stellar and I mean that
- He'll never get that picture made. - Why?
Wally's days were up twenty years ago.
You know Woody Katz is only doing it for a favor?
It was very nice and he seems so sweet.
And Wally.. Mr. Brown.. is a very close friendof my Aunt's, so..
Settle down, Betty. Don't get me wrong. I love Wally.
I ought to. I was married to him for ten years.
And I love actors, all actors.
We just get a little catty sometimes.
Now we want to take you across and introduce you to a director..
who's a head above the rest
He's got a project that you would kill!
Knock it right out of the park.
Cut it! Thank you very much, Carol. I'm coming back.
Checking the gate? Checking the gate!
Thank you! Will tell you what happens next!
Thank you very much, for coming in I know how busy you are.
Oh, are you kidding me? I love this script!
but there's no way they're gonna let me cast this thing until I've seen everyone.
- I'll let you know as soon as I can. - I know you will.
Cause my manager's gonna be bugging you day and night.. and me.
It'll be my pleasure. Now, get out of here.
Oh, you're just so cruel to me..
don't forget me. I'm the girl that's going to play this part.
- Bring her in. - Camilla Rhodes next.
Sylvia North's story, Camilla Rhodes, take one.
Ok, here we go.. Let's lock it up, please!
Cindy.. Adam wants to see Jason.
Did you want to tell me something, Adam?
- Don't stop! - What is it? What do you see?!
- Those men in front, in the car. - Do you know them?!
See, I told you there was nothing to be afraid of!
I switched apartments with her. She's in 17.
It's down in the end on the left.
She hasn't been around for a few days..
- Oh.. we'll leave her a note. - I'll go with you.
She's still got some of my stuff.
You go ahead. I've got to get that.
I guess you're not Diane Selwyn.
Still no one's home I guess.
C'mon help me in. Come on.
I know what you have to do,
You don't wear it in the house.
Just I'm looking at myself again..
I'll take it off to sleep.
And you don't have to sleep on that couch.
No it's not. This is a huge bed.
C'mon, just get in and get a good night sleep.
It's more confortable than the couch, isn't it?
I shouldn't have let you sleep on the couch last night.
Have you ever done this before?
- No, no.. - It's ok, it's ok.
It's 2 o'clock It's 2 o'clock in the morning.
And yet we hear a band.
If we want to hear a clarinette..
Sient le son du trombon in sourdine.
el club Silencio les presenta la llorona de Los Angeles:
- Where have you been? - What do you want?
C'mon Diane, it's been three weeks.
I put your dishes in that box.
Ah, by the way, those two detectives came by again, looking for you.
What was that you were saying, beautiful?
